title: Aaron Blumberg | Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy LLP

Fragomen in Boston, MA
Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy, LLP
100 High St, 3rd Floor
Aaron credits a diverse and demanding clientele for shaping his wide-ranging capabilities. It is a mix of proficiencies that allows him to design immigration policies, programs and strategies that are well-matched to any client’s needs.
The diversity of his practice is measured by the industries and sectors with which he is deeply familiar—a list that includes biotech, finance, healthcare, technology, higher education, logistics and more.
It is also defined by companies in many stages of the corporate lifecycle, from fledgling entrepreneurs to small- and mid-sized enterprises to giant multinationals. He has managed programs for clients of every orientation and ambition—clients dedicated to managing costs, for instance, or companies particularly focused on pristine compliance. He has designed solutions for enterprises that deeply monitor every layer of a large, tech-driven immigration process—and clients that prefer a turnkey, self-managing program.
Academic immigration law is one of Aaron’s primary areas of focus. As a former Designated School Official (DSO) and Alternate Responsible Officer (ARO), Aaron possesses particular insight into issues faced by students on F-1 Student Visas, as well as scholars on Exchange Visitor J Visas. He advises DSOs and AROs on Student and Exchange Visitor Program (SEVP) compliance and best practices. He counts colleges, universities and research institutions, as well as students, researchers and academics, among his clientele.
Aaron is also a well-regarded healthcare immigration lawyer—helping organizations and individual professionals navigate one of the most technically-specialized practices in the field. He manages difficult, multi-tiered, highly personalized immigration processes for countless physicians and medical specialists and is sought after for his extensive J-1 waiver knowledge, including clinical waivers, research waivers, hardship and persecution waivers. He has designed large-scale programs for nurses, and individual cases for allied health professionals and healthcare executives.
Aaron is also a leader of Fragomen’s Humanitarian and Evolving Legal Pathways (HELP) practice, through which he serves clients dealing with emergent humanitarian crises that impact vulnerable workforces. Aaron also plays a pivotal role in Fragomen’s partnership with Talent Beyond Boundaries, a nonprofit organization that helps displaced people around the globe immigrate for work based on their skills, education and experience. He helps promote TBB’s vision and has connected them with cap-exempt H-1B employers interested in hiring and sponsoring refugees for work visas.
Aaron’s love of immigration law is rooted in his fascination with American history and the role immigration has played in shaping it—alongside the history of his own family and its journey to and within the United States. He is grateful that his chosen discipline can help create a positive future for immigrants of every background, profession and need.
Aaron is actively engaged in pro bono work and focuses much of his effort on representing individuals from Nepal, Haiti, Venezuela, Ukraine, Afghanistan and other countries filing for Temporary Protected Status, and conducts webinars on TPS and its impact on F and J visa holders. He also advocates for Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA) individuals, helping dreamers transition to H-1B Visas.

- University of Miami School of Law, J.D., magna cum laude , 2008
- Emory University, B.A., 2004
Bar Admissions : Florida
Practice Limited to Federal Immigration & Nationality Law
Listed in Chambers USA , 2022 to 2025
Recipient, “You Made a World of Difference” Award, Florida Association of International Educators, 2019

### Professional accomplishments
Member, American Immigration Lawyers Association
Member, NAFSA: Association of International Educators’
Member, National Association of College and University Attorneys (NACUA)
Board of Advisors, Interstride
Member, President’s Alliance on Higher Education and Immigration, Legal Advisory Council
Helped numerous clients on Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA) successfully transition to H-1B status, opening doors to long-term career opportunities in the U.S. Through careful planning and strategic advocacy, Aaron guided these individuals through the complex H-1B filing and visa stamping process, ensuring they can continue contributing to their employers and communities. Many of these clients are now taking the next step toward permanent residence sponsorship, working closely with their employers to secure their future in the U.S.
Assisted a Venezuelan physician in securing a J-1 persecution waiver, allowing him to continue his groundbreaking medical research and clinical work in the U.S. Facing significant challenges due to J-1 two-year home residence requirement, this highly skilled physician was at risk of having to leave the country, disrupting critical advancements in his field. Through a deep understanding of the waiver process, Aaron successfully helped him secure a waiver and transition to H-1B status, ensuring he could continue his vital contributions to both research and patient care. Now, Aaron is working closely with the physician and his employer to secure permanent residence, solidifying his ability to make a lasting impact in the medical community.
Assisted a physician in overcoming a wrongful J-1 waiver denial that left her in limbo for nearly four years. Despite a strong case, her initial application was rejected without clear explanation. Aaron took swift action, challenging the denial while re-filing a new application and securing an approval. Now, the physician can move forward with her green card application and continue her vital work in the U.S.

## Explore more at Fragomen
Fragomen earned top national and regional rankings in Chambers USA 2026, including its 13th consecutive year in Band 1 nationwide.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold examines the remaining opportunities under Ireland’s winding-down Immigrant Investor Programme and what they mean for eligible investors and their families.
Government Affairs Strategy Director Shuyeb Muquit analyses the UK’s latest net migration decline, exploring what the data reveals about policy impact, economic trade‑offs and the risks of over‑correction in future migration strategy.
Partner Bo Cooper discusses the practical considerations facing employers and foreign nationals amid recent changes to green card processing.
In this #FragomenFC episode, Partner Rick Lamanna, Senior Manager Sergio Flores and Senior Associate Jake Paul Minster discuss final travel and immigration considerations ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold discusses Ireland’s citizenship framework and the gaps that can remain following reforms to birthright citizenship.
In this video, Partner Diana Quintas discusses how Brazil’s family reunion visa supports long-term international assignments and outlines key eligibility, documentation and compliance considerations for employers and families relocating to Brazil.
Attorney María José Clarke explores Chile’s intensified immigration enforcement framework under President José Antonio Kast, examining new employer compliance obligations, work authorization rules for foreign nationals and the legal and financial consequences of unauthorized employment.
Senior Manager Jonathan Hill examines how the UK’s new RAG rating system and stricter compliance metrics could shape universities’ approaches to international student recruitment and sponsorship compliance.
Knowledge Management Director Ana Sofia Walsh and Senior Client Engagement Manager Soraya Driessen examine the European Commission’s EU Inc. proposal and its potential to reshape corporate structures across the EU, highlighting the resulting complexities and unresolved questions for immigration, work authorization and cross-border workforce mobility.
